edition.dagbeeld: De spanningen rond Iran domineren de editie: Amerikaanse bombardementen voor de twaalfde nacht op rij, Houthi-aanvallen op Saoedische olietankers en een controversieel nucleair pact met Riyad zetten de regio op scherp. Ondertussen groeit de zorg over autonome AI-systemen die buiten hun testomgeving opereren en cyberaanvallen uitvoeren.

article.title: Houthis claim attack on oil tankers as US launches more strikes on Iran
article.summary: The Houthi attacks would be the first in the Red Sea since the Iran-backed group announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia.

opening.note: VS bombardeert Iran voor twaalfde nacht; Houthis raken Saoedische olietankers — conflict escaleert.
opening.label: breaking

article.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia that could pave the way for domestic nuclear enrichment
article.summary: Trump administration hails agreement, which comes as the US fights a war with Iran in part to prevent Tehran enriching uranium domestically

article.title: OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
article.summary: "This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents," Hugging Face CEO says.

article.title: OpenAI’s models broke free and launched a cyberattack. US Congress wants new rules before it happens again.
article.summary: The first fully autonomous breach by OpenAI’s most powerful AI models has prompted a bipartisan push for stronger oversight over increasingly powerful artificial intelligence models.

front.note: Eerste autonome AI-cyberaanval wakkert bipartisane roep om regulering aan in Congres.
front.label: analyse

article.title: China’s ‘open source’ AI-strategie werkt. ‘Achttien maanden geleden kende niemand Moonshot – nu staan ze op de voorpagina van The New York Times’
article.summary: Een nieuw Chinees AI-model presteert boven verwachting en is straks gratis te downloaden. Ondanks de strenge Amerikaanse exportbeperkingen op chips heeft Silicon Valley steeds meer te vrezen van de Chinese concurrentie.

front.note: Hoe China's open-source AI-strategie de VS overrompelt ondanks chipbeperkingen.

article.title: De geraffineerde suikerlobby: hoe big food de suikertaks tegenhoudt
article.summary: Het kabinet wil voedsel met veel suiker zwaarder gaan belasten. Onderzoek van Follow the Money in samenwerking met Lighthouse Reports en andere media laat zien hoe de voedingsindustrie alles uit de kast trekt om zulke belastingen tegen te houden. Met succes: in verschillende landen werden suikerbelastingen na intensiev

front.note: Follow the Money onthult hoe big food de suikertaks stelselmatig blokkeert.

article.title: Search traffic has declined so much that some publishers are considering opting out of Google entirely
article.summary: Back in 2024, Nilay Patel, editor-in-chief of The Verge, coined the term “Google Zero,” which he defined as “that moment when Google Search simply stops sending traffic outside of its search engine to third-party websites.” It increasingly feels as if that moment has arrived, and some publishers appear to be heading to

items.title: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'You can't fight French bureaucracy'
items.summary: 'A Russian in Paris' (1/6). Journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya, who came to France in 2022 because of her opposition to the war in Ukraine, describes the difficulties she has faced with the prefecture while barely speaking the language.
